LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND JOHN MURPHY'S MOTION OPPOSING DESTINEE OMARA'S MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT. THE COURT NOTES THAT THIS MATTER WAS CONTESTED LATE. NO ARGUMENT IS HELD, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DESTINEE OMARA'S APPLICATION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE - AND THE MOTION OPPOSING THE APPLICATION IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER. CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA. DEPUTY: NOT PRESENT. MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-18-2024 Continued To Apr-21-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion Opposing For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Aug-16-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Antonia Holguin, Esq. of West Coast Trial Lawyers APLC , Tel. 213.927.3700 specially appearing for Plaintiff Victoria Brignon;
Sabrina Berdux, Esq. of the Office of the City Attorney, Tel. 415.640.3969, appearing for Moving Party/Defendants City and County of San Francisco and John Murphy;
Shirley Bridwell, Esq. of Ford Walker Haggerty & Behar LLP, Tel, 310.662.3422, appearing for Defendant Destinee O'Mara.

Hearing re: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco Motion Opposing For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement.

The matter is contested. Argument is held. At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendants CCSF and John Murphy's motion contesting the Omara settlement is granted.

While the contesting party has the burden of proof on whether a settlement is entered into in good faith, substantial evidence must still be present for the court to sanction a "good faith settlement." (CCP 877.6(d) ["The party asserting the lack of good faith shall have the burden of proof on that issue."]; Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1350, n.6. [settlors must provide substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of its liability].)

When determining whether a settlement was entered into in good faith the court should take into account a rough approximation of plaintiff's total recovery and defendants' proportionate liability, the amount paid in settlement, and recognize that a settlor should pay less in settlement than if he were found liable after trial. The court should also consider the financial condition of the settlor, the existence of insurance, and any evidence of collusion. (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc. (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.)

Here, the settlors do not roughly approximate plaintiff's total recovery. The court notes, however, that plaintiff's statement of damages claims $6,000,000 in emotional and pain and suffering damages. Omara shares a large majority or complete liability. She admits that she ran the red light. (Berdux Decl., Ex. B [Omara Depo., 39:9-15, 43:4-10, 53:12-17].) CCSF refers to Omara's declaration and claims that it lacks sufficient information regarding her financial condition. The court does not see that declaration on file. In any event, given the gaping chasm between plaintiff's claimed damages, on the one hand, and the amount of the settlement and Omara's proportionate fault, on the other hand, there is not substantial evidence for the court to reasonably conclude that the settlement is in good faith.

Ms. Holguin is to prepare and submit a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. CRC 3.1312 compliance is required.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Matter Not Reported. Bailiff: Kwong. (302/RCE)

MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion Opposing Destinee Omara'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Jun-17-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance via Zoom:
Michele Messenger, Esq., Tel. 619.908.1101, appearing for Defendant/Cross-Defendant/Cross-Complainant Destinee O'Mara;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Hearing re: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND JOHN MURPHY's Motion Opposing Destinee Omara'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement.

The Court notes that this matter was contested late. No argument is held. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Destinee Omara's application for determination of good faith settlement is denied without prejudice - and the motion opposing the application is granted.

Omara drove a vehicle that collided with a San Francisco police vehicle, allegedly after she ran a red light at 2:20 a.m. Plaintiff was Omara's passenger.

As Omara recites, factors in determining whether a settlement is in good faith include: "(1) a rough approximation of the plaintiff's total recovery, (2) the settlor's proportionate liability, (3) the amount paid in settlement, (4) financial condition and insurance policy limits of the settling defendant." (Memo. 4:3-8, citing Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.)

Here, the court has not been provided enough information to enable a Tech-Bilt determination. Omara provides no approximation of plaintiff's total recovery and the City has received "no global demands" from plaintiff. (Berdux Dec. 2:9.) Both sides argue the other's liability, but provide no proportion (e.g., 50/50). Omara's counsel says she agreed to pay $25,000 insurance limits (Messenger Dec. 2:14-15), but neither side provides any information regarding Omara's financial condition.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Not present. Matter not reported.
(302/RBU)
